text.title: Security Architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Internal services trust based on VPC membership; no mTLS or signed token between services.
antipattern.fix: Add mTLS / signed-token middleware; reject unauthenticated calls regardless of source.

antipattern.symptom: GET /orders returns orders across tenants for an authenticated user.
antipattern.root-cause: r2 violated; authz check forgotten on a new endpoint.
antipattern.fix: Enforce tenant filter at ORM / RLS layer; add e2e tests proving cross-tenant requests return 404.

antipattern.symptom: DATABASE_URL with password baked into Docker image.
antipattern.fix: Move to managed secret store; rotate; remove from image; add secret scanner pre-commit.

antipattern.symptom: Security spec lists controls but no STRIDE table; missing categories surface in audit.
antipattern.fix: Author STRIDE per asset before approving spec.

antipattern.symptom: Repo has no SAST / SCA / secret scanning; vulnerabilities discovered in prod.
antipattern.fix: Wire SAST + SCA + secret + IaC scanners into CI; block merge on high severity.
